source: Claude gap analysis 2026-06-11 (Track C — one brain, many faces)
title: Multi-conversation persistence: conversations survive restarts and resume by id
current_state: T-142 gave conversations an id, but they live only in process memory — exit kills the thread. /newchat starts fresh; nothing resumes. The daemon (pi_daemon.py) keeps ONE agent warm with ONE message list.
target_state: SQLite tables conversations(id, title, mode, created_at, last_active_at) + conversation_turns(conversation_id, idx, role, content_json, ts). self.messages loads from / persists to the active conversation. New REPL commands: 'chats' lists recent 10 with titles (title = Groq one-liner of first exchange), 'resume <id>' restores messages within budget, '/newchat' unchanged. This is the storage foundation every channel (T-187..T-190) routes through.
migration_plan: 1. Schema migration in _init_sqlite (additive, IF NOT EXISTS).
2. Persist each turn's canonical Turn dicts (post-T-161 shape) as JSON at the same point L1 logging fires — one write boundary, not two.
3. Load path: resume <id> → deserialize within ctx budget → self.messages; stamp self.conversation_id.
4. Title generation lazy via router tier='cheap' on second turn.

5. Tests: write→restart(simulated new agent)→resume → golden-conversation coherence check passes across the boundary; /newchat creates a new row; tool_use turns round-trip exactly (the divergence class).
risk_notes: The serialization boundary MUST be the T-161 canonical one — do not invent a second shape. Gate test extension required. memory/ + pi_agent.py are RISK_FLAGGED → interactive.
